description: Search the public web with DuckDuckGo, fetch raw pages, and extract readable text. Use only for public/external information; internal cluster or private data should use other sidecars.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Run a DuckDuckGo text search and return titles, URLs, and snippets.

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (capped at 10).

tools.returns: Formatted search results with title, URL, and snippet per result.
tools.whenToUse: Use when the user asks about current events, public documentation, external APIs, or facts not present in the conversation context.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for internal Kubernetes state, private repositories, secrets, or tasks that can be answered from already-provided context.

tools.name: fetch_url
tools.description: Fetch the raw HTTP body of a public URL and return it as text.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Public http:// or https:// URL. Private/internal IPs and hostnames are blocked.

tools.returns: Raw response body text, truncated to a safe limit.
tools.whenToUse: Use when you need the exact markup or raw content of a specific public page.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for HTML pages where you only need human-readable text; prefer extract_text. Do not use for internal endpoints.

tools.name: extract_text
tools.description: Fetch a public URL and return the human-readable text content with HTML stripped.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Public http:// or https:// URL. Private/internal IPs and hostnames are blocked.

tools.returns: Clean, readable text extracted from the page body.
tools.whenToUse: Use when you need to read article content, documentation prose, or page text without parsing HTML.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when you need raw HTML/headers or when the page is an API returning structured data.
